stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant large language model (LLM).

stories.title: Nvidia bets $150B on Taiwan as Trump's plan to make US an AI hub backfires
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Nvidia plans a massive $150 billion annual investment to establish Taiwan as a global AI hub.
The investment is framed as a counter-narrative or consequence of previous US policy regarding AI centralization.
The story highlights geopolitical and economic shifts in the global AI supply chain.

stories.title: US Space Force confirms SpaceX will build sensor-to-shooter targeting network
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The US Space Force has confirmed a contract with SpaceX to develop a sensor-to-shooter targeting network.
The military emphasizes the need for the network to achieve both speed and scale in its operations.
This development signifies a major integration of commercial space technology into critical military targeting infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The tags confirm its relevance within the transformer, llama, and safetensors ecosystems.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets and accelerates performance on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: YouTube will let you ask AI to make a custom video feed
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: YouTube is introducing a new AI feature allowing users to generate custom video feeds.
Users can prompt the AI using descriptions related to interests, moods, or specific topics.
These personalized feeds can be pinned to the top of the YouTube homepage for easy access.

stories.title: CubePart: An Open-Vocabulary Part-Controllable 3D Generator
stories.author: Tinghui Zhou
bullet_points: CubePart is a new generative framework for creating 3D meshes with open-vocabulary and part-controllable features.
It addresses a key limitation in current 3D generation models by explicitly exposing part structure as a control signal.
This capability is highly relevant for applications like gaming and simulation, where semantic part decomposition is crucial for animation and physics.

stories.title: Bias Leaves a Gradient Trail: Label-Free Bias Identification via Gradient Probes on Concept Decompositions
stories.author: Jae Hee Lee
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, label-free, post-hoc method to identify spurious concepts (bias) in frozen vision models.
Addresses a critical limitation in current bias mitigation by not requiring curated datasets or specific bias labels.
Focuses on detecting biases that cause models to fail under distribution shift, even when in-distribution accuracy is high.

stories.title: YouTube to automatically label AI-generated videos
stories.author: nopg
bullet_points: YouTube is implementing automatic labeling for AI-generated videos.
This feature aims to increase transparency regarding synthetic media on the platform.
The move signals a growing industry effort to combat misinformation and deepfakes.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face.
The associated pipeline is focused on text generation, indicating its application in generative AI.
The tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its foundation in modern deep learning frameworks.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models offline on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux versions of this feature is planned for the near future.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within Ollama.
The feature bridges the gap between open-source models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Facebook launches a ‘Plus’ subscription that gives you extra features
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Meta is globally rolling out its 'Plus' premium subscription service across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp.
The subscription model is expanding to include testing for Meta AI features.
This move aligns Meta with the industry trend of tech companies introducing tiered subscription plans.

stories.title: Websites have a new way to spy on visitors: analyzing their SSD activity
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Websites can now monitor visitor activity by analyzing Solid State Drive (SSD) usage.
This surveillance capability can be implemented using straightforward JavaScript within a web browser.
The development represents a new, subtle vector for tracking user behavior online.

stories.title: Mystery GPS jammer in Iran becomes test for NASA satellites’ capabilities
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: NASA science satellites are being utilized to detect and locate sources of GPS interference.
The incident involves a mystery GPS jammer operating in Iran.
This demonstrates the dual-use capability of NASA's satellite technology for surveillance and interference detection.

stories.title: "Little red dot" in early Universe is a naked supermassive black hole
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Astronomers have identified a supermassive black hole in the early universe.
This black hole is exceptionally dominant, comprising over two-thirds of its host object's mass.
The discovery relates to early universe cosmology and black hole formation.

stories.title: Beyond Binary: Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation with Physics-Grounded Contact Representation
stories.author: Toru Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the 'sim-to-real' gap in dexterous manipulation, a major bottleneck in robotics.
Proposes a novel approach, 'Center-of-Pressure,' to handle rich, high-dimensional contact data from simulation.
Aims to overcome the limitation of existing methods that simplify tactile data into coarse features.

stories.title: Calibrating Conservatism for Scalable Oversight
stories.author: Mohsen Bayati
bullet_points: Addresses the fundamental control problem of maintaining human oversight over highly capable, autonomous AI systems.
Introduces 'Calibrated Collective Oversight' (CCO) as a new framework for scalable oversight.
CCO aggregates diverse auxiliary scoring functions to provide oversight with statistical guarantees, addressing limitations of current heuristic methods.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research focuses on multimodal meta-verification to improve the reliability of large language models that incorporate visual data.
OmniVerifier-M1 introduces a method that uses verifier-generated rationales, rather than just binary decisions, for verification.
The work explores how to effectively integrate meta-verification feedback into the training process of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: CaMBRAIN: Real-time, Continuous EEG Inference with Causal State Space Models
stories.author: Mubarak Shah
bullet_points: CaMBRAIN introduces a new approach for real-time, continuous EEG inference using Causal State Space Models.
It addresses limitations of existing EEG deep learning models, specifically the quadratic scaling issue associated with attention mechanisms.
The method overcomes the challenge of processing long, continuous EEG signals that are typically constrained by fixed-length sliding windows.

stories.title: Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation for LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Yuzhi Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation (SGSD) to enhance LLM reasoning.
Moves beyond traditional methods that rely on trusted, static teacher information (e.g., reference answers).
Proposes using an experience-derived 'skill bank' as the source of supervision, acknowledging potential relevance/misleading data.

stories.title: Do Agents Need Semantic Metadata? A Comparative Study in Agentic Data Retrieval
stories.author: Natasha Noy
bullet_points: The paper investigates the necessity of traditional semantic metadata (like schema.org) in the context of autonomous AI agents.
It addresses the challenge posed by Large Language Models (LLMs) that can navigate unstructured web data, potentially bypassing the need for strict metadata.
The research is relevant to the future of machine-actionable data and adherence to FAIR principles in agentic workflows.

stories.title: Rethinking Memory as Continuously Evolving Connectivity
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes FluxMem, a novel memory framework for LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of static memory in current memory-augmented LLMs.
Models memory as a continuously evolving heterogeneous graph to handle dynamic environments.

stories.title: SwarmHarness: Skill-Based Task Routing via Decentralized Incentive-Aligned AI Agent Networks
stories.author: Edwin Jose
bullet_points: SwarmHarness proposes a decentralized protocol to utilize vast amounts of unused compute resources (GPUs, edge devices).
It addresses limitations of existing systems, such as reliance on central coordinators or heavy blockchain infrastructure.
The core innovation is an incentive-aligned AI agent network designed to ensure safe and profitable resource sharing.

stories.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces PEFT-Arena, a new benchmark for evaluating Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) methods.
Addresses a gap in current PEFT evaluations by focusing on the stability-plasticity dilemma.
Measures the trade-off between achieving target-task adaptation and retaining general pretrained capabilities.

stories.title: Beyond Binary: Sim-to-Real Dexterous Manipulation with Physics-Grounded Contact Representation
stories.author: Toru Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the 'sim-to-real' gap in reinforcement learning for dexterous manipulation, a major challenge in robotics.
Proposes a novel method, 'Center-of-Pressure,' to handle rich, high-dimensional contact data (like touch) directly from simulation.
Aims to overcome the limitation of existing methods that simplify tactile data into coarse features, enabling more complex real-world tasks.

stories.title: Affective Music Recommendation: A Rollout-Based World Model for Offline Preference Optimization
stories.author: Laurent Charlin
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of recommending music based on affective (emotional) state, a key problem in functional music applications.
Proposes AMRS, a system utilizing a 'Rollout-Based World Model' to optimize offline preference without requiring real-time emotional feedback.
Highlights the ethical constraints of online emotional experimentation, particularly in clinical and health-wellness settings.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for reliable, fine-grained verification in multimodal large language models (LLMs) as visual data becomes central.
It introduces 'multimodal meta-verification,' which uses detailed rationales generated by verifiers instead of simple pass/fail decisions.
The work focuses on effectively integrating this meta-verification feedback into the training process of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: Ω-QVLA: Robust Quantization for Vision-Language-Action Models via Composite Rotation and Per-step Scaling
stories.author: Peng Lu
bullet_points: The research addresses the high computational cost of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which integrate perception, reasoning, and control.
The core problem is the difficulty in efficiently deploying these large models on edge devices due to their multi-billion parameter size and complex action heads.
The proposed solution, Ω-QVLA, introduces robust quantization techniques using Composite Rotation and Per-step Scaling to improve deployment feasibility.

stories.title: CaMBRAIN: Real-time, Continuous EEG Inference with Causal State Space Models
stories.author: Mubarak Shah
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in applying deep learning to long-duration Electroencephalography (EEG) data.
It tackles the quadratic scaling issue of attention-based models when processing long EEG sequences.
The proposed solution, CaMBRAIN, utilizes Causal State Space Models for real-time, continuous EEG inference.

stories.title: Rethinking Memory as Continuously Evolving Connectivity
stories.author: Ningyu Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes FluxMem, a new memory framework for LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of static memory repositories in dynamic agentic environments.
Models memory as a heterogeneous graph whose topology evolves progressively.

stories.title: PEFT-Arena: Understanding Parameter-Efficient Finetuning from a Stability-Plasticity Perspective
stories.author: Weiyang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces PEFT-Arena, a new benchmark for evaluating Parameter-Efficient Finetuning (PEFT) methods.
Shifts focus from solely measuring downstream accuracy to assessing the trade-off between task adaptation and capability retention (stability-plasticity).
Addresses a gap in current LLM evaluation by quantifying how well fine-tuned models retain their general pretrained knowledge.

stories.title: VLMs May Not Globally Enhance Human Alignment over LLMs During Natural Reading
stories.author: Zitong Lu
bullet_points: The study investigates whether Vision-Language Models (VLMs) improve text representations to be more human-like compared to Large Language Models (LLMs) during natural reading.
The research isolates the effect of multimodal training history by comparing matched LLM and VLM pairs in a strictly text-only setting.
The findings suggest that VLM training may not universally enhance human alignment over LLMs when analyzing text alone.

stories.title: Self-Improving Language Models with Bidirectional Evolutionary Search
stories.author: Yilun Du
bullet_points: Proposes 'Bidirectional Evolutionary Search' to enhance self-improving language models and agentic systems.
Addresses limitations of existing search methods (like best-of-N and tree search) which are restricted by sparse verification signals and autoregressive expansion.
Aims to improve exploration in language models by moving beyond reliance solely on high-probability regions.

stories.title: Personal Visual Memory from Explicit and Implicit Evidence
stories.author: Yuheng Li
bullet_points: The research addresses the gap in personalized AI memory systems, which are currently too text-centric.
It proposes methods to leverage visual information (images) for user-specific memory, moving beyond simple text captions.
The work focuses on capturing both explicit (e.g., recurring entities) and implicit personal information embedded within images.

stories.title: OmniVerifier-M1: Multimodal Meta-Verifier with Explicit Structured Recalibration
stories.author: Ling Yang
bullet_points: Introduces OmniVerifier-M1, a multimodal meta-verifier designed to improve the reliability of multimodal LLMs.
The research focuses on leveraging detailed, verifier-generated rationales instead of simple decision signals for verification.
It explores methods to effectively incorporate meta-verification feedback into the training process of multimodal verifiers.

stories.title: Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation for LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Yuzhi Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Skill-Conditioned Gated Self-Distillation (SGSD) to enhance LLM reasoning.
Moves beyond traditional methods that rely on trusted, privileged information (PI) like reference answers.
Proposes using an experience-derived 'skill bank' as a source of PI, acknowledging potential irrelevance or misleading nature.

stories.title: Can Large Language Models Handle Discourse Particles? A Case Study of Colloquial Malay
stories.author: Xi Chen
bullet_points: The research investigates the ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to process discourse particles, which are vital for human-like communication (conveying emotion and intention).
A key gap identified is the lack of comprehensive understanding of LLMs' performance with these particles, especially outside of high-resource languages.
The study specifically addresses the underrepresentation of Southeast Asian languages, using Colloquial Malay as a case study.

stories.title: The Abstraction Gap in Vision-Language Causal Reasoning
stories.author: Mohammad Rashedul Hasan
bullet_points: Addresses a critical limitation in Vision-Language Models (VLMs): current evaluations conflate fluent language with actual causal reasoning.
Proposes a novel 'dual-probe methodology' to decouple linguistic plausibility from faithful causal reasoning.
Introduces the 'Abstraction Gap (AG) metric' to quantify the performance difference between these two properties across various VLMs.

stories.title: Top API Gateways for AI Applications and Agentic Workflows (2026 Developer Guide)
stories.author: hadil
bullet_points: The article focuses on API Gateways, a critical infrastructure component for production AI applications.
It targets a specific, growing need: managing complex Agentic Workflows in AI systems.
The guide aims to solve a common industry problem—the failure point of AI applications post-prototyping.

stories.title: Cómo Prevenir Loops de Razonamiento en Agentes de IA y No Desperdiciar Tokens
stories.author: elizabethfuentes12
bullet_points: The article addresses the problem of reasoning loops in AI agents.
These loops occur when an AI agent repeatedly calls the same tool.
The focus is on preventing these loops to conserve computational resources (tokens).

stories.title: We Measured LLM Prompt Caching in Production — Same Prompt, 0% to 91% Hit Rates
stories.author: sm1ck
bullet_points: The article investigates LLM prompt caching performance in a production environment.
It reports variable hit rates for identical prompts, ranging from 0% to 91%.
The context involves an AI companion bot where a consistent ~5K-token prefix is present in every chat turn.
